title: Neutralize an Acid Spill
description: Neutralize an acid spill with staged evidence gates, safe escalation, and a verified re-check.

dialogue.text: Acid spill on bench. Choose your approach, but do not rush: isolate the area, PPE on, and keep ignition sources and unprotected hands away.
Pick a response path. Main path uses a structured spill kit run; alternate path is manual neutralization with tighter checks.

options.text: Main path: use spill kit workflow.
Alternate path: manual neutralization and strip checks.

dialogue.text: Deploy absorbent perimeter, add neutralizer in small increments, and watch reaction intensity. Stop immediately if vapor or heat spikes.

options.text: Run `adjust-ph` to document controlled neutralization before manual verification.
Kit run complete; verify with pH strip.

dialogue.text: Sprinkle baking soda slowly from the edge inward and avoid splashing. Keep containment in place until fizzing slows, then proceed to verification.

options.text: Manual neutralization complete; proceed to pH verification.

dialogue.text: Interpret the verification reading. Pass only when strip indicates near-neutral range and no active reaction remains.

options.text: Verification passed: spill zone is neutral and controlled.
Verification failed: still acidic/basic or active fizzing.

dialogue.text: Recovery branch: back out, refresh PPE, re-contain the zone, and re-run neutralization in smaller doses. If instability persists after one retry, escalate to supervisor and lock the station.

options.text: Escalated and locked out bench safely pending review.

dialogue.text: Bench safety restored. You used evidence-backed verification and followed a stop-work recovery path instead of forcing a risky finish.
